tiled.body: Subduction megathrusts violate the isotropic-kernel and point-source assumptions that generic ETAS makes for great earthquakes, so a Chilean tile needs Slab2 geometry, possibly anisotropic / finite-fault triggering, and Chilean-network Mc. Each regime tile is fit and CSEP-tested on its own catalog; the global map is the mosaic of these tiles, fit fine and reported coarse.

encoder.title: CNN spatial-context encoder
encoder.body: A convolutional encoder turns static geophysical layers (Slab2 depth/dip, distance-to-fault, GNSS strain) into a per-cell context tensor that conditions the point-process intensity. It supplies learned spatial anisotropy and covariate ingestion — the two ETAS gaps where learning helps — while the temporal triggering stays in the Hawkes skeleton, so the backbone remains interpretable and CSEP-testable. The CNN is an encoder, not a standalone forecaster.

note.title: Fit fine, report coarse
note.body: ETAS is fit and CSEP-tested on a fine 0.1° × 0.1° grid with 0.1-magnitude bins, the resolution at which the S-test and M-test can resolve where and what size. The UI then aggregates that fine grid into H3 hexbins / tectonic polygons for display.

gated.title: Gated challengers
gated.body: Machine-learned challengers are feature-flagged and ship only on positive, significant pseudo-prospective information gain over ETAS: a RECAST-class neural temporal point process , which improves on temporal ETAS only when the training catalog is large, otherwise it merely matches ; and tidal / GNSS / hydrological covariates, encoded as a regularized multiplier that shrinks to ~0 where there is no signal.
gated.detectionNote: Machine-learned detection and phase-picking models (PhaseNet, EQTransformer, SeisBench) are used only to build a more complete input catalog. Detection is not forecasting, and that line stays explicit.

pipeline.title: The daily offline pipeline
pipeline.intro: Heavy compute runs once per day, offline, on a single CPU host; the runtime is stateless and read-only. A strict forecast-clock drives each daily issue, so temporal leakage is structurally impossible.

steps: Snapshot inputs at issue time: pull ComCat and the regional network for the slice up to t only, and persist the exact catalog state as an immutable, versioned artifact so the forecast is byte-reproducible months later.
Run hygiene — time-varying Mc → Mw homogenization → dual-catalog declustering — with post-mainshock incompleteness correction if a recent large event is present.
Fit / update region-refit ETAS on the fine grid (seconds-to-minutes of CPU, no GPU); enforce α < β and reject any supercritical n ≥ 1 fit.

Generate: simulate at least 10,000 synthetic next-day catalogs into bounded probabilities for 1d / 2d / 7d at M*, with P10 / median / P90 bounds; emit both a gridded-rate and a catalog-based forecast.
Calibrate (isotonic / Platt), QA-gate, write one compact artifact (a few hundred KB to a few MB, gzipped), log it immutably, and serve it stateless and read-only.
flow.qaTitle: Operational QA gate — degrade visibly, never silently
flow.qaBody: A single bad, duplicated, or retracted event near M* can swing a public probability. Publication is gated on input-catalog sanity, artifact integrity, and a rolling-window N-test drift monitor on the forecaster itself. On an FDSN outage or a failed run, the product serves 'unavailable' with a staleness banner — it never silently serves a stale or corrupted artifact.

diagram.svgDesc: A top-to-bottom flow of five stages: external data feeds, the offline daily job, the compact artifact, the thin API, and the static SPA.
diagram.caption: Heavy compute runs once per day, offline; the runtime is stateless and read-only.

feeds.title: Data feeds (external, read-only, public)
feeds.body: USGS ComCat / ISC / IRIS-EarthScope FDSN → recent catalog (last N days)

job.title: Offline daily job (single host, CPU)
job.body: ingest → QC / dedup → Mc & magnitude homogenization → declustering (dual-catalog) → fit / condition ETAS (+ R-J fallback) → simulate ensemble → per-cell rate field → optimistic / expected / pessimistic bounds → rolling CSEP stats

artifact.title: Artifact (committed / served static, gzipped)
artifact.body: forecast_YYYY-MM-DD.json(.gz) + H3 rate arrays: per-cell λ for {1d, 2d, 7d} × {lo, exp, hi}; baseline λ; metadata / timestamp; N/S/M/L summary + reliability points; coverage mask

spa.title: SPA (Vite + React + TS, i18n EN→ES, light / dark)
spa.body: Intro · Problem · Methodology · Implementation · Back-analysis · Monitoring (world MapLibre + deck.gl H3 field, country zoom, SVG summary; legend · horizon · baseline · calibration · staleness)

arrow.render: fetch + render the chosen horizon × bound

ba.title: Back-analysis
ba.lede: Retrospective, pseudo-prospective CSEP scores across tectonically diverse regions — reported in full, including where the model does not win.
ba.honesty: Skill is established only by winning comparison tests against real baselines. Passing consistency (calibration) tests is necessary but not sufficient. Every region × horizon cell is reported, including failures — selective reporting is itself the bias CSEP exists to prevent.

ridgecrest.title: Worked example — Ridgecrest 2019
ridgecrest.body: A roughly 3% first-week forecast preceded the M7.1 by about 34 hours . A ~3% forecast is not wrong when the 3% outcome occurs: a single outcome neither validates nor invalidates a probabilistic forecast. The reliability diagram, accumulated over many forecasts, is what judges the model.

ba.sampleBanner: Illustrative SAMPLE results — not real CSEP scores. Replaced byte-for-byte by the offline pyCSEP evaluation job.
ba.error: Could not load the back-analysis report: {{message}}

cell.overRejectTip: Poisson grid tests over-reject during aftershock sequences (over-dispersion). This grid-test result is paired with the catalog-based result, which relaxes the Poisson assumption.

comparison.title: Comparison tests (skill vs baselines)
comparison.note: Skill is claimed only when IGPE is positive with a T-test confidence interval excluding zero, corroborated by the Wilcoxon test.

calibration.tooltip: The green / amber / red triad describes MODEL QUALITY from CSEP tests — never earthquake danger.

name.CL: Conditional likelihood test (preferred over raw L)

csep.necessaryNotSufficient: Passing consistency tests calibrates one model; it never establishes skill. Skill lives in the comparison tests against ETAS and smoothed seismicity. Information gain is in nats, not bits.

monitoring.title: Monitoring
monitoring.lede: The live daily forecast field: where the conditional rate is elevated relative to its own long-term baseline, with an explicit horizon and magnitude threshold.
monitoring.framing: This is not an alarm map. It shows where the conditional rate is elevated relative to its own long-term baseline — a continuous probability field with an explicit horizon and magnitude threshold, always shown with its uncertainty bounds.
monitoring.error: Could not load the forecast artifact: {{message}}
monitoring.sampleBanner: Illustrative SAMPLE forecast — not a real forecast. Replaced byte-for-byte by the daily publish job.

hi.label: Pessimistic (P90)
hi.tip: The pessimistic 90th-percentile bound — a plausible bad case.
hi.caption: The pessimistic view shows a plausible bad case, not a prediction.

legend.rampAria: Perceptually-uniform sequential colour ramp (low to high probability)
legend.title: {{bound}} · P(≥1 event ≥ M{{m}}) over {{days}} day(s)
legend.caption: Per cell (~{{area}} km²), over {{days}} day(s), for one or more events of M ≥ {{m}}.
legend.notSafe: A blank cell means no forecast or out of coverage — never that an area is safe.

map.noWebgl: This browser cannot render the WebGL field. Switch to the no-map summary view for the same data.

summary.head: {{bound}} · most-elevated cells · P(≥1 event ≥ M{{m}}) over {{days}} day(s)
summary.ratioTip: Forecast probability divided by the long-term baseline probability.
summary.baselineNote: Each cell shows the forecast next to its long-term baseline, both as a ratio and an absolute probability — a large ratio on a near-zero baseline still reads 'still very unlikely'.
summary.empty: No cells above the rate floor for this slice — the field is at background everywhere shown.

detail.honesty: The bounds are a real epistemic + aleatory range, not a cosmetic interval. A large ratio on a small baseline is still small in absolute terms.

staleness.failed: The latest daily run did not complete — the field is desaturated and may be stale.

monitoring.coverageNote: {{count}} cell(s) are explicitly out of validated coverage and are hatched on the map — blank never means safe.
monitoring.provenanceNote: Artifact schema {{schema}} · issued {{issued}}. Served static, read-only; no inference per request.
